mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which historically precedes broad-based earnings upgrades and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, an acceleration in real consumer credit growth indicates resilient household demand that offsets higher borrowing costs, sustaining equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS rises and RSAFS accelerates simultaneously, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of financial stress (VIX > 30).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through analyst forecasts.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.20, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105, capacity utilization reaches levels that historically trigger supply constraints and input cost inflation, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 105, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment and stable federal funds rates signals a healthy labor market without aggressive monetary tightening, which historically supports risk appetite and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate statistically significant excess returns compared to a buy-and-hold benchmark, or the interaction term's marginal effect becomes negative during periods of elevated inflation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating housing starts, rising real consumer credit growth amplifies the wealth effect on household balance sheets, driving discretionary spending and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rising for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.15 over a 3-year test period.
